CLIMATE ADAPTATION ASSESSMENT
Report No. ENV-2025-03

Prepared by: Dr. Sarah Mitchell, Division Head
City of Portland, Bureau of Environmental Services
Date: February 28, 2025

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This report assesses climate-related flood risks in the Pearl District
and recommends mitigation strategies for 2025-2030.

KEY FINDINGS

1. The Pearl District faces significant flood risk due to aging stormwater
   infrastructure. A 100-year flood event would cause an estimated $120M
   in property damage under current conditions.

2. Stormwater system upgrades are needed by 2027 to meet projected
   rainfall increases of 15-20% over the next decade.

3. Estimated capital cost for required upgrades: $45M over 5 years.

4. Green infrastructure (bioswales, permeable surfaces) should be expanded
   by 12% in central neighborhoods to reduce runoff by 30%.

5. Three critical pump stations (Pearl, NW 23rd, Burnside) require
   immediate capacity upgrades at a cost of $8.2M.

RECOMMENDATIONS

- Prioritize pump station upgrades in FY2025-26 budget cycle
- Allocate $9M annually for stormwater infrastructure improvements
- Partner with Portland State University on green infrastructure monitoring
- Update floodplain maps by December 2025

REFERENCES
Portland Climate Action Plan 2023
NOAA Regional Rainfall Projections (2024 update)
FEMA Flood Insurance Rate Maps, Multnomah County
